title: Telling My Katbot To Be A Contrarian at 73K
author: claytantor
selftext:
Just wanted to share this because it’s been a total game-changer for my focus. I used to stare at charts for 6 hours, get bored, and then entry into a terrible position just to 'feel something.'

I’ve been using an **OpenClaw** agent integrated with **Katbot AI**, and it’s simplified my life. Look at the screenshot—I literally just told the bot: *'If BTC goes over 73k, open a short.'* That’s it. I didn’t have to set 5 alarms, I didn’t have to fiddle with exchange UI, and I didn’t have to worry about my brain changing its mind at the last second. It just monitors the price in the background and executes when the criteria are met.

If you’re like me and your biggest trading enemy is your own impulsivity/boredom, this setup is worth looking into. It’s super easy to get running.

1. **Get OpenClaw:** You can host it yourself or use the [Hostinger 1-click install](https://www.hostinger.com/vps/openclaw-hosting) 
2. **Install the Skill:** Inside OpenClaw, go to Skills → Add Skill and search for `katbot-trading` or grab it from [ClawHub](https://clawhub.ai/claytantor/katbot-trading).
3. **Run Onboarding:** Run the setup script to link your Hyperliquid account: `python3 ~/.openclaw/workspace/katbot-trading/tools/katbot_onboard.py`
4. **Authorize & Talk:** Copy your "Agent Wallet" address to your [Hyperliquid API settings](https://app.hyperliquid.xyz), and you’re ready to start chatting with your bot.

**Full Guide:** [Katbot OpenClaw Integration Docs](https://docs.katbot.ai/guides/openclaw-katbot-integration/)

comments.author: dogazine4570
comments.body:
Automation for execution discipline is honestly underrated. The biggest edge for a lot of retail traders isn’t some secret indicator — it’s removing the impulse to “click something” out of boredom.

That said, a couple things to think about if you’re auto‑shorting a breakout level like 73k:

- Are you defining it as a wick above 73k or a confirmed close?  
- Are you adding any volatility filter (e.g., only trigger if move > X% in Y minutes)?  
- Is position sizing fixed or dynamic based on ATR/liquidity?  

Blind contrarian entries can get steamrolled in strong momentum environments, especially if funding and open interest are expanding in the breakout direction.

Also make sure your API permissions are tightly scoped (no withdrawal rights), and test slippage assumptions during high‑vol spikes. Automation reduces emotional errors, but it can amplify logic errors if the rules aren’t robust.

Overall though, anything that stops revenge trades and boredom entries is a win. The real edge is consistency.

comments.author: bjxxjj
comments.body:
That actually sounds more useful for discipline than for pure automation. The biggest edge for most of us isn’t prediction, it’s removing impulsive clicks when we’re bored.

One thing I’d be curious about: how are you handling risk management with that setup? Is the bot also setting predefined SL/TP or position sizing rules, or is it just executing the trigger? A contrarian short above 73k can make sense in certain liquidity conditions, but without volatility-based sizing it could get ugly fast.

I’ve found that automating *conditions* works best when it’s tied to a broader plan (e.g., short only if funding is extreme + RSI divergence + liquidity sweep), not just a single price level. Otherwise you’re basically hard-coding a narrative.

That said, removing the “stare at charts for 6 hours and revenge trade” cycle is huge. If this keeps you from overtrading, that alone is probably +EV long term. Just make sure the bot is executing a system, not replacing one emotional trigger with another.
